title: Seeded Recursive Backtracking Generation · Language Clinic: Backtracker Invariant
summary: Generation chooses visited neighbors, recursive depth overflows silently, random state is global, and completion is confused with connectivity. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the backtracker invariant increment in seeded iterative and recursive backtracker. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain a current cell, visited set, frontier stack or call path, reciprocal passages, and a progress invariant.
State vertices, edges, representation, traversal work, memory, and grid-specific bounds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-generation-backtracker-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-generation-backtracker-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_generation_backtracker_3(cells: int = 48, visited: int = 48, carved_edges: int = 47, stack_peak: int = 21, replay_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or visited < 0 or carved_edges < 0:
        return False, visited, "generation-count"
    if visited != cells:
        return False, visited, "unvisited-cell"
    if carved_edges != cells - 1:
        return False, carved_edges, "tree-shape"
    if stack_peak < 1 or not replay_match:
        return False, stack_peak, "replay-invariant"
    assert visited == cells and carved_edges == cells - 1
    return True, visited, "seeded-backtracker"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-backtracker-invariant-1teaq3
# Competency: maze-backtracker-invariant.
# Case 8510: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def evidence_maze_backtracker_invariant_1teaq3(cells: int = 48, visited: int = 48, carved_edges: int = 47, stack_peak: int = 21, replay_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1teaq3 = "1teaq3-3qaet1-8510"
    assert evidence_variant_1teaq3
    evidence_axes_1teaq3 = (
        "grid1teaq3",
        "topology8510",
        "search3qaet1",
        "event1teaq38510",
        "access85101teaq3",
        "recovery3qaet18510",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1teaq3)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or visited < 0 or carved_edges < 0:
        return False, visited, "generation-count"
    if visited != cells:
        return False, visited, "unvisited-cell"
    if carved_edges != cells - 1:
        return False, carved_edges, "tree-shape"
    if stack_peak < 1 or not replay_match:
        return False, stack_peak, "replay-invariant"
    assert visited == cells and carved_edges == cells - 1
    return True, visited, "seeded-backtracker"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Backtracker Invariant: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 6960 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seeded backtracking progress, completion, topology, depth, and replay; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain a current cell, visited set, frontier stack or call path, reciprocal passages, and a progress invariant.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Backtracker Invariant model · Theory Backtracker Invar….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Backtracker Invariant model · Theory Backtracker Invar…, keep Backtracker Invariant connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain a current cell, visited set, frontier stack or call path, reciprocal passages, and a progress invariant.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Backtracker Invariant model · Theory Backtracker Invar…, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Backtracker Invariant precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Backtracker Invariant model · Theory Backtracker Invar…, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 6960 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seeded backtracking progress, completion, topology, depth, and replay; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain a current cell, visited set, frontier stack or call path, reciprocal passages, and a progress invariant.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Backtracker Invariant model · Theory Backtracker Invar…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Randomly removing walls eventually creates a valid maze.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-backtracker-invariant-1teaq3.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-backtracker-invariant-1teaq3(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_backtracker_invariant_1teaq3\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cells < 1 or visited < 0 or carved_edges < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?visited != cells)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?carved_edges != cells - 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stack_peak < 1 or not replay_match)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
